# Huawei New-Gen OceanStor # A800 High-Performance AI Storage ![AI Brain with Data Centers and Distributed Servers for High-Performance Storage](/system/doc-content/561bef56-2a7e-4e3b-ad07-812baebe66a6/images/f54e9dc0f77e66ec199f5cb5faca1deeafa1f21e7345aea4186feb8cb5101fe3.jpg) The image visually encapsulates the core themes of the Huawei OceanStor A800 document through symbolic and architectural elements. At its center, a stylized AI brain (representing AI workloads) contains data center icons, directly linking storage infrastructure to AI processing. The surrounding server racks and chip-like components illustrate the distributed, scalable architecture emphasized in the document (e.g., Omni-Expansion to 512 controllers). This visual metaphor reinforces the 'AI Native' design by positioning storage as an integral part of the AI brain’s functionality, while the networked server layout supports the document’s claims of flexible scalability and ultra performance for large AI model training/inference. The image thus serves as a conceptual bridge between the technical features (data/control plane separation, 500 GB/s bandwidth) and the practical application of high-performance AI storage. ![Huawei OceanStor A800 High-Performance AI Storage Controller Enclosure](/system/doc-content/561bef56-2a7e-4e3b-ad07-812baebe66a6/images/ec9fda9dfc10582189fd47952b567818df8bbd4f4b2d779b6374d8ead1971618.jpg) The image displays the physical hardware of the Huawei OceanStor A800, a distributed storage system explicitly designed for AI workloads. This visual directly supports the document's focus on the A800 as a 'new-gen high-performance AI storage' by providing a tangible representation of the product that underpins the described technical capabilities. The enclosure's robust, enterprise-grade design and prominent Huawei branding align with the document's emphasis on scalable, high-performance infrastructure for large AI models. The image visually reinforces the document's claims about the A800's role in enabling ultra performance (e.g., 10 million IOPS per controller enclosure) and flexible scalability (supporting up to 512 controllers), as the physical unit is the foundation for these architectural and performance attributes. Additionally, the modern, modular aesthetic of the enclosure reflects the 'Omni-Expansion' and 'AI Native' themes highlighted in the document, reinforcing the product's purpose in accelerating large AI model training and inference. Ultra Performance - Omni-Expansion AI Native Huawei OceanStor A800 is the new-gen high-performance distributed storage designed for AI. Its innovative data and control plane separation architecture enables ultra performance and accelerates large AI model training and inference. OceanStor A800 offers 500 GB/s bandwidth and 10 million IOPS per controller enclosure, 4x faster training set loading, and 3x faster training resumption from checkpoints than the leading alternative. Huawei OceanStor A800 high-performance AI storage also supports flexible scalability, with a single cluster able to scale out to 512 controllers and EB-level storage capacity expansion needed for the smooth evolution of multimodal models with trillions or tens of trillions of parameters. OceanStor A800 supports new data paradigms, like vectors, with its built-in high-performance vector retrieval engine that accelerates retrieval from large-capacity vector knowledge bases and reduces AI hallucinations. It also provides multi-level KV cache to reduce inference latency while improving cost-effectiveness. This enables a single storage system to meet the data processing needs of the entire AI training and inference process. ![Huawei OceanStor A800 high-performance AI all-flash distributed storage system](/system/doc-content/561bef56-2a7e-4e3b-ad07-812baebe66a6/images/6ae92df95a11224e43711315c04182ce92490a49a708b71076783f85a87caf4c.jpg) The image depicts a large-scale, modular black storage system with Huawei branding, representing the physical hardware of the Huawei OceanStor A800. This visual directly supports the document’s focus on the A800 as a new-gen, high-performance AI storage solution. The document highlights the A800’s ultra-performance (500 GB/s bandwidth, 10 million IOPS per controller), AI-native architecture, and scalability (up to 512 controllers, EB-level capacity). The image’s modular, extensive design visually embodies the 'Omni-Expansion' and scalability features, while its all-flash infrastructure aligns with the 'all-flash high-performance' description in the product introduction. By showing the tangible, large-scale system, the image reinforces the technical claims about the A800’s ability to handle massive AI workloads (e.g., training large multimodal models) and its role in enabling efficient AI training/inference processes. OceanStor A800 high-performance AI storage # Product Introduction - OceanStor A800 is an all-flash high-performance AI storage product that delivers excellent performance, high capacity, and elastic scalability based on brand-new hardware. It integrates disks and controllers, with each controller enclosure supporting 64 high-speed NVMe SSDs and providing raw capacity ranging from 245.76 TB to 983.04 TB. With ultimate scalability, a single cluster can scale out to 512 controllers, reaching EB-level capacity. - Based on the brand-new data and control plane separation architecture, OceanStor A800 high-performance AI storage provides up to 500 GB/s bandwidth and 10 million IOPS. A single storage system accelerates the entire AI process, from data ingestion to training and inference. OceanStor A800 is widely applicable to financial AI models, regional intelligent computing centers, AI computing power centers, scientific research platforms, autonomous driving platforms, and more. # Product Features # $\triangleleft$ Ultra Performance for Higher AI Cluster Computing Power Utilization OceanStor A800 uses NPUDirect Storage (NDS) to build an optimal performance path from NPUs to data storage, bypassing complex protocol stacks and eliminating the need for data copy, improving performance by $40\%$ . New-gen OceanStor A800 AI storage uses the DataTurbo high-performance file acceleration engine to directly read data from and write data to the storage system in kernel mode. Kernel-mode caching enables memory data mobility and halves memory usage. OceanStor A800 offers 500 GB/s bandwidth per controller enclosure, over 100 TB/s aggregated bandwidth per cluster, read and write for TB-level checkpoints in seconds when training models with trillions of parameters, and fast resumable training from checkpoints. Based on a global unified data view, OceanStor A800 uses DPUs to offload the general computing power of CPUs. Data read and write operations are processed by DPUs, bypassing the CPUs and memory. The host can thus directly connect to disks to read and write data. A single controller enclosure provides 10 million IOPS and a cluster provides hundreds of millions of IOPS, with four times higher data loading efficiency than the leading alternative. # $\triangleleft$ More Flexible Multi-Dimensional Expansion Based on the brand-new hardware architecture, new-gen OceanStor A800 AI storage delivers flexible scalability with both scale-out and scale-up capabilities. A single cluster can scale out to up to 512 controllers and EB-level storage capacity, ensuring high performance and capacity density. In addition, computing cards, such as DPU and GPU accelerator cards*, can be inserted into the back end of the array. The storage cluster can scale up to a maximum of 4,096 cards, together to provide 14,400 TFLOPS of computing power and accelerate data processing. It can also carry multiple offloaded functions, including encryption, compression, and vector retrieval. A single new-gen OceanStor A800 AI storage system can meet diverse ecosystem requirements. It is compatible with file, object, and vector, among other protocols, and supports the entire large AI model development process, such as data ingestion, preprocessing, model training, and deployment and inference. OceanStor A800 eliminates the need for data copy and accelerates the iteration and development of large AI models. New-gen OceanStor A800 AI storage provides SmartQuota. With this file system quota technology, system administrators can manage and control the storage resources. By limiting the disk space that can be used by specified subjects, SmartQuota prevents excessive resource occupation and maximizes the value of storage space. # $\triangleleft$ More Efficient Inference with AI-Native Data Paradigms The data paradigms supported by new-gen OceanStor A800 AI storage range from NAS, and objects to new vectors and graph adaptive retrieval engines. OceanStor A800 enables high-performance retrieval from massive amounts of multimodal knowledge. The solution's competitive advantage lies in its inference based on RAG knowledge bases and Unified Cache. OceanStor A800 provides a built-in large-capacity AI knowledge base with hundreds of millions of data records for 3x higher QPS than the leading alternative. This enables faster knowledge base creation and more accurate queries. OceanStor A800 significantly accelerates inference thanks to its Unified Cache feature, which offers multi-level KV cache from on-chip memory to SSDs. This establishes a PB-level global shared resource pool for KV cache, enabling flexible allocation across multiple compute nodes and xPUs and eliminating repeated computing. This slashes the Time to First Token (TTFT) by $78\%$ while improving the throughput by $60\%$ . It also uses sparse attention algorithms for retrieval to compress tokens to enable the inference of ultra-long sequences beyond the context window, ultimately making inference smoother, faster, and more cost-effective. Key Specifications
ModelOceanStor A800
Hardware ArchitectureDisk and controller integration
Maximum Raw Capacity per Controller Enclosure983.04 TB
Height of Each Controller Enclosure8 U
Controllers per Controller Enclosure2
Disks per Controller Enclosure64
Processors per ControllerFour Kunpeng 920 processors
Maximum Memory per Controller1024 GB
Data Disk TypePalm-sized NVMe SSD
Network Type25/100 Gbit/s TCP/IP25/100/200 Gbit/s RoCE200 Gbit/s InfiniBand
Key FeatureCluster (HyperScale), quota (SmartQuota), Quality of Service (SmartQoS), multi-protocol interworking (SmartInterworking)*, end-to-end data integrity check (DIF)
Enclosure Dimensions (H x W x D)352.8 mm × 447 mm × 952.2 mm
Operating Temperature-60 m to +1800 m altitude: 5°C to 35°C1800 m to 3000 m altitude: The max. temperature threshold decreases by 1°C for every altitude increase of 220 m
Operating Humidity10% to 90% RH
The table supports the section summary by providing the specific hardware specifications that underpin the storage system's ability to handle massive AI workloads. The 983.04 TB raw capacity per enclosure substantiates the summary's claim of a built-in large-capacity AI knowledge base. Additionally, the four Kunpeng 920 processors and 1024 GB of memory provide the computational power necessary to achieve high QPS and support sparse attention algorithms for ultra-long sequences. The support for high-speed network interfaces and HyperScale cluster architecture further validates the infrastructure required for high-performance retrieval and flexible scalability across compute nodes. * If this feature is required, contact the sales personnel. This visual element supports the document's context by fulfilling the call-to-action at the end of the text: 'To learn more about Huawei storage, please contact your local Huawei office or visit the Huawei Enterprise website.' The QR code provides a practical means for readers to access additional resources (e.g., product documentation, case studies) related to the OceanStor A800's AI-native features (such as RAG knowledge bases, unified cache, and inference acceleration) highlighted in the section summary. By offering a quick access point to Huawei's external resources, the image reinforces the product's market positioning as a leading solution for AI storage needs, aligning with the document's goal of promoting the A800's technical advantages.
